title: Melton Mowbray Wellington Memorial
crew_summary: 7 killed (1 survived); pilot Flg Off. Norman Owen, DFC

external_narratives.summary: Detailed account of Wellington LN281's loss on 13 August 1944. F/O Norman Owen DFC was an experienced pilot who had completed 36 operational sorties as a Lancaster pilot with 207 Squadron at RAF Spilsby before becoming an instructor at 14 OTU Market Harborough. The starboard engine failed during a cross-country exercise and bombing detail; too low to bail out, Owen attempted an emergency circuit on one engine but the aircraft stalled and crashed between Saxby Road and Thorpe Road, four miles from Melton airfield.
key_details: Owen completed 36 ops with 207 Sqn at RAF Spilsby
DFC for full operational tour on Lancasters
Crashed between Saxby Road and Thorpe Road
Sgt George Henry Raby was sole survivor

external_narratives.summary: RAFA branch page documenting the Wellington LN281 memorial and the crew who died. Provides context about the memorial's significance to the Melton Mowbray community.
International war heritage database entry for the Wellington LN281 memorial at Melton Mowbray, with location details and photographs.
HistoryFare research articles covering the Wellington LN281 crash, 14 OTU operations, and the broader context of training accidents at RAF Melton Mowbray during WW2.

local_contacts.role: Local authority responsible for memorial upkeep
